//: The catch-all rewrite below is what makes the SPA work on deep links. It does NOT
clobber the prerendered blog pages from scripts/prerender-blog.mjs: Vercel applies
`rewrites` only AFTER checking the filesystem, so dist/blog/<slug>/index.html wins
for /blog/<slug> and the rewrite never fires. (Note `vite preview` does NOT behave
this way — its fallback beats the file — so a local preview is not a valid test of
this. Verify against a real deployment, see below.)
cleanUrls + trailingSlash:false pin the canonical URL form to /blog/<slug> — no
trailing slash, no .html — which is exactly what <link rel='canonical'> emits. Left
to defaults, /blog/foo and /blog/foo/ can both resolve and split the same page.
AFTER DEPLOY, verify the whole point of this actually works:
-> want ~1700 words. If you get ~130, you are serving the SPA shell and every AI
crawler still sees a blank page.
Also confirm Cloudflare (or any proxy) is not blocking AI bots at the edge, which
happens BEFORE robots.txt is ever read:  curl -I -A GPTBot https://jobsynk.co/
